questions.question: Which keyword is used to inherit in Java?
questions.answer: `extends`

questions.question: How many classes can a Java class extend directly?
questions.answer: One (single inheritance)

questions.question: What is method overriding?
options: Creating two methods with same name
Subclass provides its own implementation of a parent method
Method with same name different params
questions.answer: Subclass provides its own implementation of a parent method

questions.question: What does `super.speak()` do?
questions.answer: Calls the parent class's `speak` method

questions.question: What does `@Override` do?
options: Compiler check — error if not actually overriding
questions.answer: Compiler check — error if not actually overriding

questions.question: All Java classes implicitly extend:
questions.answer: `java.lang.Object`

questions.question: What is polymorphism?
options: One interface, many forms — same variable holds different subtype objects
questions.answer: One interface, many forms — same variable holds different subtype objects

questions.question: What is an abstract class?
options: Class that cannot be instantiated and may have abstract methods
questions.answer: Class that cannot be instantiated and may have abstract methods

questions.question: What is an abstract method?
options: A method without a body — subclasses must implement it
questions.answer: A method without a body — subclasses must implement it

questions.question: Does the parent constructor automatically get called in Java?
options: Yes — if not specified, `super()` is called implicitly as the first statement
questions.answer: Yes — if not specified, `super()` is called implicitly as the first statement

questions.question: Can a Java class implement multiple interfaces?
questions.answer: Yes

questions.question: What is an interface vs abstract class?
options: Interface has no instance fields and supports multiple inheritance; abstract class can have fields and one parent
questions.answer: Interface has no instance fields and supports multiple inheritance; abstract class can have fields and one parent

questions.question: What does dynamic dispatch mean?
options: JVM selects the correct overridden method at runtime based on actual object type
questions.answer: JVM selects the correct overridden method at runtime based on actual object type

questions.question: What is a `ClassCastException`?
options: Runtime exception when an invalid cast is attempted on a reference type
questions.answer: Runtime exception when an invalid cast is attempted on a reference type

questions.question: What is `final` on a class?
questions.answer: Cannot be subclassed

questions.question: What does a `default` method in an interface do?
options: Provides a default implementation — implementing classes optionally override
questions.answer: Provides a default implementation — implementing classes optionally override

questions.question: What is the Liskov Substitution Principle?
options: Subclass objects should be usable wherever parent objects are expected without breaking behavior
questions.answer: Subclass objects should be usable wherever parent objects are expected without breaking behavior

questions.question: What does `sealed` on an abstract class do (Java 17+)?
options: Restricts which classes can extend it — permits list must be specified
questions.answer: Restricts which classes can extend it — permits list must be specified

questions.question: What is covariant return type in Java?
options: Returning a different type each call
Override can return a subtype of the parent method's return type
questions.answer: Override can return a subtype of the parent method's return type

questions.question: Can interfaces have private methods (Java 9+)?
options: Yes — private methods in interfaces for shared helper logic among default methods
questions.answer: Yes — private methods in interfaces for shared helper logic among default methods

sublevels.theory:
**Diamond problem in Java:** solved via interfaces (only one class parent). If two interfaces have the same `default` method, the implementing class must override it.

**Visitor pattern:** double dispatch workaround for type-based operations without `instanceof`:
```java
interface Visitor { void visit(Circle c); void visit(Rect r); }
interface Shape { void accept(Visitor v); }
```

**`MethodHandles.Lookup`:** reflective access at record/class level — more performant than `Method.invoke()`.

**Marker interfaces:** interfaces with no methods (e.g., `Serializable`, `Cloneable`) used to signal capability.

questions.question: How does Java resolve diamond problem with interfaces?
options: If two interfaces have same default method, implementing class must override to resolve ambiguity
questions.answer: If two interfaces have same default method, implementing class must override to resolve ambiguity

questions.question: What is the Visitor pattern?
options: Double dispatch pattern for type-specific operations without instanceof chains
questions.answer: Double dispatch pattern for type-specific operations without instanceof chains

questions.question: What is a marker interface?
options: An interface with no methods used to tag a class with a capability (e.g., Serializable)
questions.answer: An interface with no methods used to tag a class with a capability (e.g., Serializable)

questions.question: What is double dispatch?
options: Choosing a method based on the runtime type of TWO objects — used in Visitor pattern
questions.answer: Choosing a method based on the runtime type of TWO objects — used in Visitor pattern

questions.question: What happens if you call a non-abstract parent method that's overridden, via a `super` reference in the wrong context?
options: You can call `super.method()` only from direct child — you can't 'skip' grandparent in Java
questions.answer: You can call `super.method()` only from direct child — you can't 'skip' grandparent in Java

questions.question: What is `java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke()` overhead?
options: 10-100x slower than direct call due to security checks and boxing — avoid in hot paths
questions.answer: 10-100x slower than direct call due to security checks and boxing — avoid in hot paths

questions.question: What is the Template Method pattern?
options: Base class defines algorithm skeleton; subclasses override specific steps without changing structure
questions.answer: Base class defines algorithm skeleton; subclasses override specific steps without changing structure

questions.question: What is `Class.getInterfaces()` useful for?
options: Retrieving the interfaces a class directly implements — used in proxy/DI frameworks
questions.answer: Retrieving the interfaces a class directly implements — used in proxy/DI frameworks

questions.question: How does JVM handle method dispatch table?
options: Using a virtual method table (vtable) per class — method calls resolved via pointer lookup
questions.answer: Using a virtual method table (vtable) per class — method calls resolved via pointer lookup

questions.question: What is `non-sealed` in Java sealed class hierarchy?
options: Opens the class for any extension again
Marks a permitted subclass that itself can be freely extended
Removes the sealed restriction from hierarchy
questions.answer: Both A and B
